description: Resource scope templates for proposed-action NEPA project requirements packages. These templates are planning guidance over current authority-family data; they do not create compliance findings or legal conclusions.

data_needs: Project name, forest, district, responsible official, and proponent
Purpose and need, proposed action, no-action baseline, and preliminary alternatives
Decision schedule, public involvement plan, and expected decision document
defensibility_checks: The NEPA level and decision pathway are explicit.
The proposed action is specific enough to support resource specialist analysis.
The project record can show how public involvement, alternatives, effects, and decision documentation were handled.
required_deliverables: Draft EA outline and administrative record index
resource_scopes.resource_name: NEPA project management and decision record
sow_tasks: Confirm purpose and need, proposed action, no-action baseline, and preliminary alternatives.
Define the decision record, public involvement, objection or appeal path, and project schedule.
Maintain an administrative record index that ties resource products to the EA sections they support.

data_needs: Federal and non-federal parcel descriptions, acres, estate interests, reservations, and outstanding rights
Appraisal, equalization, title evidence, mineral reservations, easements, and exchange agreement status
Public interest determination and lands/realty case file milestones
defensibility_checks: The exchange case file supports public interest, equal value, title, and reservation findings.
Federal land actions are separated from non-federal acquisition actions.
Implementation conditions needed for closing are visible as requirements, not implied conclusions.
required_deliverables: Land exchange feasibility and public-interest memorandum
Draft realty implementation and closing checklist
resource_scopes.resource_name: Lands, realty, and land exchange case requirements
sow_tasks: Compile parcel, ownership, estate, access, reservation, and title facts for all lands entering or leaving federal ownership.
Identify appraisal, equalization, exchange agreement, notice, closing, and public-interest determination requirements.
Coordinate realty conditions with resource mitigation, easements, deed restrictions, and decision documentation.

data_needs: Forest plan profile, forest unit, district, geographic areas, management areas, and overlays
Forest Plan standards, guidelines, desired conditions, objectives, and suitability direction potentially touched by the action
Plan consistency table or crosswalk from proposed action elements to applicable plan components
defensibility_checks: Applicable Forest Plan components are identified before compliance conclusions are made.
Project geography and overlays are tied to plan-source evidence.
Any plan amendment or exception question is isolated as a blocker.
resource_scopes.resource_name: Forest Plan consistency and NFMA requirements
sow_tasks: Resolve the forest plan profile and project geography from the intake.
Identify management areas, geographic areas, overlays, and applicable plan components.
Prepare the Forest Plan consistency matrix needed for the EA and decision record.

data_needs: Listed, proposed, candidate, sensitive, and species-of-conservation-concern screens
Habitat, survey, timing, conservation measure, and consultation assumptions
Biological assessment/evaluation need and consultation pathway
defensibility_checks: Species and habitat effects are separated from background species mentions.
ESA, sensitive species, eagle, migratory bird, and EFH triggers are each explicitly resolved.
Consultation or no-effect/no-impact rationale is citation-ready.
required_deliverables: Biological assessment or biological evaluation scope
resource_scopes.resource_name: Wildlife, fish, botany, and species consultation
sow_tasks: Screen the action area for listed species, sensitive species, species of conservation concern, eagles, migratory birds, EFH, and habitat triggers.
Define survey, consultation, conservation measure, and biological evaluation deliverables.
Tie each species conclusion to action-area facts and source evidence.

data_needs: Area of potential effects, known sites, survey status, and confidentiality limits
SHPO/THPO, tribal consultation, treaty rights, sacred sites, and traditional cultural property indicators
Avoidance, mitigation, MOA, monitoring, or inadvertent discovery requirements
defensibility_checks: Cultural-resource and tribal-interest triggers are not collapsed into a generic heritage note.
Consultation status, confidentiality limits, and mitigation commitments are explicit.
Resource products can support NHPA/SHPO and tribal-consultation record requirements.
required_deliverables: Cultural resource survey and eligibility scope
resource_scopes.resource_name: Cultural resources, SHPO/THPO, and tribal consultation
sow_tasks: Define the APE, cultural-resource inventory status, and consultation path.
Identify tribal, treaty, sacred-site, THPO, and confidentiality requirements.
List mitigation, monitoring, avoidance, MOA, and inadvertent discovery deliverables if triggered.

data_needs: Waters of the United States, wetlands, floodplains, riparian areas, aquatic habitat, and groundwater/drinking-water indicators
Dredge, fill, discharge, crossing, culvert, road, trail, construction, fish, trout, and mitigation assumptions
Permit, certification, avoidance, minimization, mitigation, aquatic habitat, and water-quality requirements
defensibility_checks: Wetland, WOTUS, floodplain, and water-quality triggers are each resolved.
Permit requirements are visible as tasks even when later found not required.
Avoidance/minimization/mitigation assumptions are tied to action elements.
resource_scopes.resource_name: Hydrology, wetlands, floodplains, and water quality
sow_tasks: Screen for wetlands, WOTUS, floodplains, riparian areas, water-quality permits, and construction-related discharge or fill.
Identify field delineation, permit, certification, avoidance, minimization, and mitigation deliverables.
Tie water-resource analysis to specific action elements, locations, and implementation commitments.

data_needs: Road, trail, access, right-of-way, special-use, closure, construction, and maintenance action elements
Roadless, recommended wilderness, WSR, national trail, special area, and recreation setting overlays
Motorized/mechanized/nonmotorized use constraints and public access commitments
defensibility_checks: Access benefits and construction effects are analyzed as separate work requirements.
Designated-area constraints are explicit before effects conclusions.
Road/trail actions are traceable to maps, design criteria, and implementation commitments.
required_deliverables: Roads, trails, and access report scope
resource_scopes.resource_name: Roads, trails, access, recreation, and designated areas
sow_tasks: Identify all road, trail, access, right-of-way, closure, special-use, and recreation action elements.
Screen designated-area constraints, including roadless, recommended wilderness, WSR, national trails, and special areas.
Define map, design, access, and implementation deliverables needed for the EA and decision record.

data_needs: Vegetation treatment, fuels, prescribed fire, timber, forest health, invasive species, pesticide/herbicide, soils, air-quality, climate, and carbon indicators
Treatment units, equipment, timing, burn/smoke assumptions, erosion control, carbon storage or sequestration context, revegetation, and restoration commitments
Air-quality, greenhouse gas, carbon, smoke, soil productivity, noxious weed, and invasive species prevention requirements
defensibility_checks: Treatment methods and affected resources are separated from background forest-health discussion.
Smoke, air, soil, invasive species, and restoration commitments are explicit when triggered.
Implementation requirements are stated as tasks and deliverables, not assumed compliance.
required_deliverables: Soils and invasive species prevention plan scope
resource_scopes.resource_name: Vegetation, fuels, soils, air quality, climate, and carbon
sow_tasks: Screen vegetation, fuels, prescribed fire, timber, soils, invasive species, pesticide/herbicide, air-quality, climate, and carbon triggers.
Define treatment-unit, method, timing, equipment, smoke, carbon, erosion, restoration, and monitoring deliverables.
Tie prevention and mitigation commitments to the action elements that create the need.

data_needs: Mineral estate, reserved minerals, mining claims, energy uses, hazardous materials, contamination, and due-diligence records
Phase I environmental site assessment, mine waste, landfill, spill, release, or recognized environmental condition indicators
Reserved rights, mineral report, reclamation, and hazardous-materials mitigation requirements
defensibility_checks: Mineral estate and hazardous-materials questions are resolved before land adjustment or ground-disturbance decisions.
Known or potential site conditions are visible as due-diligence tasks.
Reserved rights and mitigation commitments are carried into realty and decision documentation.
required_deliverables: Mineral potential and reserved-rights review scope
sow_tasks: Screen mineral estate, reserved rights, mining, energy, hazardous materials, contamination, and due-diligence indicators.
Define mineral report, Phase I/site condition, disclosure, mitigation, and implementation deliverables.
Coordinate minerals and hazardous-materials requirements with realty and resource mitigation commitments.

data_needs: Stakeholders, tribes, counties, agencies, adjacent landowners, permittees, and interested publics
Scoping method, comment tracking, response-to-comments workflow, and objection or appeal path
Notice, publication, meeting, and administrative review requirements
defensibility_checks: Public involvement is planned as a record requirement, not a summary afterthought.
Consultation and coordination parties are linked to the resources they affect.
Comment response, objection, and decision notice responsibilities are explicit.
required_deliverables: Comment response and administrative review checklist
resource_scopes.resource_name: Public involvement, coordination, and administrative review
sow_tasks: Identify required and discretionary scoping, consultation, coordination, notice, and comment-response tasks.
Build a contact matrix for tribes, agencies, counties, adjacent landowners, permittees, and interested publics.
Define objection, appeal, decision notice, and final agency action documentation needs.
